No appearances, not contested, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Parsons Government Services, Inc. (FKA Parsons Infrastructure & Technology Group, Inc.)'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. The Court considered Exhibits A and B to the Hopwood Declaration submitted in support of the motion. The Court also considered Exhibits B, C, and D to the Rose Declaration submitted in opposition to the motion. (See C.C.P. section 437c(c).)

Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that Decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to Defendant. (See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.)

Plaintiffs' responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One describe that Decedent worked at the Arco Refinery in Carson, CA. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 7:6.) Plaintiffs responded that Defendant's laborers swept and shoveled asbestos-containing insulation, insulating cements, refractory, mastics, gaskets and packing debris and dust on at least a daily basis in Decedent's work areas. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 7:21-22.) Defendant fails to address the six co-worker witnesses who are still alive that Plaintiffs identified in their responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories: Richard Rolaff of Cushing, Oklahoma; Taylor Gibson of Westminster, California; Wayne Hunt, c/o Brayton Purcell LLP; Patrick Doyle, c/o Brayton Purcell LLP; Hoot Gibson, address currently unknown; and John Jones, address currently unknown. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 7:23-26.)

Plaintiffs' responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One also describe that Decedent worked at the Standard Oil Refinery in El Segundo, CA. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 7:28-8:1.) Plaintiffs responded that Defendant was one of the general contractors that employed laborers who were sweeping and cleaning asbestos-containing debris at the site in Decedent's work areas. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 8:16-17.) Defendant fails to address the two co-worker witnesses who are still alive that Plaintiffs identified in their responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories: Steve Stevens, address currently unknown; and Wayne Hunt, c/o Brayton Purcell LLP. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 8:18-19, 22.) As such, Defendant's motion fails to establish the full breadth of Plaintiffs' claims.

Plaintiffs' responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One finally list Decedent as a person with knowledge of facts pertaining to product identification. (Hopwood Decl., Ex. B at 12:8-9.) As to the former deposition testimony of Decedent in the Stanley Puchosic personal injury asbestos case, admissibility is an issue for the trial judge. In any event, Defendant failed to object to the depositions showing that Defendant exposed Decedent to asbestos at Standard Oil (Rose Decl., Ex. B at 55:7, 55:11-14, 55:18-20, 56:4-20, 57:2-9, 12-13) and Arco (Rose Decl., Ex. B at 54:12-14, Ex. C at 661:17-25, 662:11-15, 22-25, Ex. D at 62:15-19, 75:21-76:11). Combined with Defendant's failure to present evidence that it deposed the co-worker witnesses, Defendant fails to sustain its burden that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence of product identification. Product identification remains a triable issue of fact. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)
